DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Rodriguez, Esq. 949-476-3785 of Valinoti, Specter & Dito LLP. for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Kristen Bennett, Esq. 703-506-2050 of Moore & Lee for Defendant SUNRISE CARLISLE LP, SUNRISE SENIOR LIVING MANAGEMENT, INC.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT SUNRISE CARLISLE LP, SUNRISE SENIOR LIVING MANAGEMENT, INC.'s DEMURRER TO PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF THE CARLISLE OF SAN FRANCISCO HOMEOWNERS' ASSOCIATION'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants Sunrise Carlisle LP and the Sunrise Senior Living Management, Inc.'s demurrer to the fifth and sixth causes of action for fraud and fraudulent accounting in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff The Carlisle of San Francisco Homeowners Association is overruled as to both causes of action. Liberally construed, both claims adequately allege actionable fraud by concealment claims. Defendants can seek more information about the claims by discovery and, if warranted, can file a summary motion that the claims lack merit.

Order is signed and an Order on stipulation to set hearing date on a motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication by Defendant within 30 days of trial is also signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Off Calendar Nov-29-2017 Continued To Jan-17-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-30-2017 Set For Order To Show Cause On Dec-19-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-29-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Mark A. Rodriguez of the law offices of Valinoti Specter & Dito LLP (949-833-9400) appears by telephone as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Kenneth L. Valinoti of the law offices of Valinoti & Dito LLP (415-986-1338 x 135) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Kristen Bennett of the law offices of Moore & Lee LLP (703-506-2050) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS SUNRISE CARLISLE LP and SUNRISE SENIOR LIVING MANAGEMENT, INC.'s DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the fourth cause of action for negligence is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Demurrer to the fifth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, and the sixth cause of action for fraud is OVERRULED. The fourth cause of action for negligence does not allege a breach of a duty independent of the party's contracts, or a duty that does not duplicate the supporting facts and the relief sought under the fifth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty. Sunrise Carlisle LP, et al are precluded from demurring as to fifth cause of action under Code Civ. Proc., 430.41 (b), because the grounds for the demurrer existed in the first complaint and were not raised with the previous demurrer. The sixth cause of action is pled with sufficient particularity for the elements of fraudulent concealment, where it is alleged that defendants concealed material facts regarding the necessity of establishing a reserve for the installation of a safe access system for defendants' personal gain and profit. ("Less specificity in pleading fraud is required "when 'it appears from the nature of the allegations that the defendant must necessarily possess full information concerning the facts of the controversy." Cansino v. Bank of America (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 1462, 1469).) Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-03-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SUNRISE CARLISLE LP, SUNRISE SENIOR LIVING MANAGEMENT, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT as to the third, fifth, sixth and eighth causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff The Carlisle of San Francisco Homeowners Association is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. The third cause of action for breach of the implied covenant does not adequately allege a breach of an implied covenant separate from an express contractual term. The fifth cause of action for negligence does not adequately allege a breach of duty independent of the parties' contracts. The sixth and eighth causes of action for negligent and intentional misrepresentations do not adequately allege a misrepresentation an actionable misrepresentation independent of the parties' contracts. If it can do so in good faith, the Association is given leave to amend to cure these deficiencies. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
